title: ranunculus geraniifolius subsp. breyninus
intro: Ranunculus geraniifolius subsp. breyninus, also known as the Alpine Buttercup, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). The Alpine Buttercup is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its potential health benefits of reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the Alpine Buttercup can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
